message.content: {"items":[{"title":"German General Social Survey – ALLBUS 1980–2022 (historical modules on attitudes toward the German Empire and Weimar period)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.14330","justification":"Includes retrospective questions and background variables used for historical analyses of attitudes toward periods of the German Reich and early 20th century German history, enabling contextual quantitative work on 1900s–1920s developments."},{"title":"European Values Study 1981–2008 (Integrated Dataset, Longitudinal File) – Germany","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12253","justification":"Contains retrospective questions and historical reference variables relevant to the interpretation of German attitudes toward institutions, nation, religion, and democracy in the context of the late German Empire, World War I, and the Weimar Republic."},{"title":"Historical Electoral Data for the German Empire and Weimar Republic, 1871–1933","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8012","justification":"Provides constituency‑level election results in the German Empire and Weimar Republic, covering Reichstag elections between 1871 and 1933; allows study of political history and electoral behavior in the 1900s, 1910s, and 1920s within the German Reich."},{"title":"German Election and Party System Data, 1890–1933","url_or_doi":"https://search.gesis.org/research_data/ZA8011","justification":"Contains longitudinal, party‑level and summary electoral indicators for elections in the German Empire and Weimar Republic, giving quantitative access to the political history of the German Reich from the turn of the century through the 1920s."},{"title":"Historical Official Statistics of Germany (HISTAT) – Population and Economy of the German Empire and Weimar Republic","url_or_doi":"https://www.gesis.org/histat/en/metadata/official-statistics/germany-empire-weimar","justification":"Offers digitized official statistics for the German Empire and Weimar Republic, including demographic, economic, and social indicators that cover the decades 1900–1929, suitable for macro‑historical analyses of the German Reich."},{"title":"Historical Official Statistics of Germany (HISTAT) – Education and Culture in the German Empire and Weimar Republic","url_or_doi":"https://www.gesis.org/histat/en/metadata/official-statistics/education-culture","justification":"Provides historical series on schooling, universities, culture, and related institutions within the German Empire and Weimar Republic, allowing researchers to analyze educational and cultural history in the early 20th‑century German Reich."},{"title":"Mikrozensus 1973–2004: Historical Regional Data for East and West Germany (with variables referring to pre‑1945 regions)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13057","justification":"Contains regional identifiers and background variables that allow linkage to historical territories of the German Reich; can be used in combination with historical data to study long‑term effects of conditions in the 1900s–1920s."},{"title":"German National Election Study – Historical Cumulation 1949–2017 (contextual variables on Weimar and Imperial legacies)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13271","justification":"While surveys start after 1945, the study includes contextual and reference variables useful to examine how memories and legacies of the German Empire, World War I, and the Weimar Republic shape later political attitudes."},{"title":"International Social Survey Programme – National Identity (Integrated File, including Germany)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12408","justification":"Enables analysis of how Germans in later decades relate to national symbols, history, and territory, with direct reference to the historical German Reich and its early 20th‑century period, useful for historically oriented identity research."},{"title":"Historical Official Statistics of Germany (HISTAT) – Labour and Social Policy in the German Empire and Weimar Republic","url_or_doi":"https://www.gesis.org/histat/en/metadata/official-statistics/labour-social-policy","justification":"Comprises time series on employment, wages, social insurance, and labour relations in the German Empire and Weimar Republic, covering 1900s–1920s and enabling quantitative studies of social and economic history of the German Reich."}]}
